text:
Once there was a peculiar fish named Felix. Felix lived in a crystal-clear lake known only to the wisps that danced at the fringes of forgotten dreams. He had a curious mind and a talent for finding lost things. One day, while scavenging near the edge, he stumbled upon a shiny object that couldn’t be mine—I hadn’t even visited that part of the lake in years. It was a watch, elegant yet worn.

Curiosity overtook him, and Felix decided to peek inside. To his wonderment, the interior glowed with an inner light. The watch had a voice that belonged to an ancient owl.

“Seeker of Lost Things,” it whispered. “You have found courage. What will you do?”

Felix decided that perhaps the least mysterious creature on the planet deserved the gift. He took it home and tucked it away in his cozy cave.

Months passed. Felix noticed the watch flickering intermittently—it was time for a power charge. One night,
